_description: Teaching loop queue -- lesson plans for the py/agent course. Each entry is one lesson/video. Modules group lessons by theme. Pick the next pending item and scaffold following CLAUDE.md phased commits.

_curriculum.title: Agentic AI: From RAG to Production
_curriculum.description: A progressive course on building AI agents with LangGraph and LangChain. Each example is one lesson and one video. Complete a module before moving to the next. Modules 1-8 cover the foundations; Modules 9-13 are advanced and production-focused.

queue.video_title: Build Your First RAG Agent in Python (Local ChromaDB)
queue.topic: Minimal RAG pipeline -- load documents, split, embed, store in ChromaDB, retrieve, generate
queue.notes: Entry point for the entire course. No prior knowledge needed. Teaches the four stages: chunk, embed, store, retrieve+generate.

queue.video_title: RAG with Cloud Vector DB + Web Search Fallback (Qdrant + Brave)
queue.topic: RAG with cloud-hosted Qdrant vector DB and a web-search fallback tool
queue.notes: Shows how to swap ChromaDB for a managed cloud store and add a Brave Search fallback when the vector store has no relevant results.

queue.video_title: Prospect Research Agent: Personalized Outreach with AI
queue.topic: Prospect research agent -- DuckDuckGo search then personalized outreach draft with confidence score
queue.notes: First practical application example. Shows how to chain search + generation into a useful product-like workflow.

queue.video_title: Build a ReAct Agent with Memory and PDF Retrieval
queue.topic: ReAct agent with conversation memory and PDF retrieval tool
queue.notes: Teaches the ReAct pattern (Yao et al. 2022): reason, act, observe, repeat. Uses LangChain tools and memory. Core pattern for all agent work.

queue.video_title: LangGraph ReAct Agent: Summarize + Critique Loop
queue.topic: LangGraph ReAct agent: PDF summarizer with a critique loop between two specialist nodes
queue.notes: Upgrades example 4 from a linear chain to a proper LangGraph StateGraph with conditional routing between a summarizer and a critic.

queue.video_title: Two-Agent Graph: Route Between Specialists with LangGraph
queue.topic: Two-agent graph: ProductQnA and OrdersAgent routed by a supervisor node
queue.notes: First multi-agent example. Introduces the supervisor pattern where a router node directs incoming messages to the correct specialist.

queue.video_title: RAG with Redis Vector Store and Query Rewriting
queue.topic: RAG backed by Redis vector store with a grader node that rewrites the query if retrieval fails
queue.notes: Shows Redis as a production-grade vector store and introduces the query-rewrite pattern when initial retrieval is not relevant enough.

queue.video_title: Idea Generation Agent: Parse and Score Business Ideas
queue.topic: Two-stage pipeline: parse the free-for-dev list then generate structured, scored ideas
queue.notes: Practical application. Shows how to chain a scraping/parsing step with an LLM generation step to produce a scored idea list.

queue.video_title: Prospect Agent with SearXNG: Privacy-First Web Search
queue.topic: Prospect agent upgraded to SearXNG (self-hosted, privacy-focused, multi-engine search)
queue.notes: Upgrades example 3 to use SearXNG instead of DuckDuckGo. Teaches how to swap search backends and why self-hosted search matters for production.

queue.video_title: Real-Time RAG: Stream LangGraph Output Token by Token
queue.topic: RAG pipeline with .stream(stream_mode='updates') for live node-by-node output
queue.notes: Teaches LangGraph's streaming API. Same pipeline as example 1 but emits events per node so users see partial results while the graph runs.

queue.video_title: Human-in-the-Loop: Pause and Resume Any AI Agent
queue.topic: HITL: interrupt() pauses the graph mid-run for yes/no approval, Command(resume=) continues from checkpoint
queue.notes: Teaches LangGraph's interrupt primitive. Critical pattern for any production agent where a human must approve before an action is taken.

queue.video_title: RAG from Scratch: Complete Tutorial Notebook (Colab)
queue.topic: Jupyter/Colab teaching workbook -- chunk, embed, store, retrieve, generate end-to-end with exercises
queue.notes: The deep-dive companion to example 1. Colab-ready, includes exercises. Teaches the full RAG pipeline with cell-by-cell explanation.

queue.video_title: Structured Output with Pydantic and LangChain
queue.topic: Search then extract a validated Pydantic model with with_structured_output()
queue.notes: Teaches how to force an LLM to return a validated Pydantic object instead of free-form text. Foundational for every example that uses typed state.

queue.video_title: Build a Text-to-SQL Agent with LangGraph
queue.topic: ReAct agent over a local SQLite DB using create_react_agent; seeds sample data on first run
queue.notes: Shows how to point a ReAct agent at a relational database. The agent writes and executes SQL queries to answer natural language questions.

queue.video_title: CrewAI Basics: Build a Research + Writer Crew
queue.topic: Multi-agent research crew with researcher + writer roles using CrewAI
queue.notes: First framework contrast example. Same multi-agent pattern as example 6 but using CrewAI's Crew/Agent/Task primitives instead of LangGraph StateGraph.

queue.video_title: Evaluate Your RAG Pipeline with RAGAS
queue.topic: RAG evaluation notebook -- faithfulness, answer relevance, context recall with RAGAS
queue.notes: First evaluation example. Teaches how to score a RAG pipeline automatically using the RAGAS library on a labeled test set.

queue.video_title: Corrective RAG (CRAG): Self-Healing Retrieval
queue.topic: Corrective RAG (CRAG) -- grade retrieved docs, rewrite query if irrelevant, fall back to web search
queue.notes: Based on CRAG paper (Yan et al. 2024). Adds a grader node that checks retrieved docs and rewrites the query or falls back to web search if they are irrelevant.

queue.video_title: Reflexion Agent: Critique Your Own Answers Until Confident
queue.topic: Reflexion agent -- generate answer, critique it with structured confidence score, revise in a loop until confident
queue.notes: Based on Reflexion paper (Shinn et al. 2023). The agent generates an answer, critiques it for accuracy and completeness, and revises until a confidence threshold is met.

queue.video_title: Multi-Agent Patterns: Supervisor, Workers, and Shared State
queue.topic: Multi-agent patterns notebook -- supervisor/worker routing, message passing, shared state
queue.notes: Deep-dive notebook on multi-agent patterns. Covers the supervisor pattern with add_messages reducer, create_react_agent workers, and conditional routing.

queue.video_title: AI Code Interpreter: Write, Run, and Fix Python Automatically
queue.topic: Code interpreter agent -- generates Python code, executes in subprocess sandbox, returns result
queue.notes: write_code node generates Python, run_code node executes it in a subprocess sandbox with 10s timeout. On error, routes back to write_code with the error message. MAX_ITERATIONS=3.

queue.video_title: AutoGen Debate: Two AI Agents Argue a Topic
queue.topic: Two AutoGen agents debate a topic -- ConversableAgent multi-agent chat
queue.notes: Framework contrast: AutoGen ConversableAgent vs LangGraph StateGraph. AutoGen manages the turn loop internally with initiate_chat; no StateGraph needed.

queue.video_title: Hybrid Search RAG: BM25 + Vector = Better Retrieval
queue.topic: Hybrid search RAG -- combine BM25 keyword search with vector similarity via Reciprocal Rank Fusion
queue.notes: EnsembleRetriever with BM25Retriever + Chroma vector store, weights=[0.5, 0.5]. Teaches RRF merging and when keyword search outperforms vector search.

queue.video_title: CrewAI Deep Dive: Agents, Tasks, Processes, and Tools
queue.topic: CrewAI patterns notebook -- agents, tasks, crews, tools, sequential vs hierarchical processes
queue.notes: 12-cell deep-dive notebook on CrewAI. Covers Agent (role/goal/backstory/tools), Task (description/expected_output), and process types. Includes LangGraph vs CrewAI comparison table.

queue.video_title: Graph RAG: Entity Extraction + Knowledge Graph Traversal
queue.topic: Graph RAG -- extract entities and relationships into a NetworkX knowledge graph, traverse for retrieval
queue.notes: LLM extracts (subject, predicate, object) triples; NetworkX DiGraph built at startup. Teaches multi-hop retrieval by traversing graph edges rather than vector similarity. Inspired by Microsoft GraphRAG.

queue.video_title: Adaptive RAG: Route Queries to the Right Retrieval Strategy
queue.topic: Adaptive RAG -- classify query complexity, route to direct LLM / vectorstore / web-search path
queue.notes: LLM classifier routes each query to the cheapest correct strategy: simple (direct LLM), vectorstore (Chroma), web (DuckDuckGo). Decision-before-retrieval vs CRAG's correction-after-retrieval.

queue.video_title: RAG Fusion: Generate Multiple Queries, Rank with RRF
queue.topic: RAG Fusion -- generate N query variants, retrieve for each, merge with Reciprocal Rank Fusion
queue.notes: LangGraph Send API fans out to N parallel retrieve_variant nodes; Annotated[list, add] merges back. Shows how Send enables map-reduce in LangGraph. Based on Lawrence 2024.

queue.video_title: Self-RAG: Three Decision Gates for Smarter Retrieval
queue.topic: Self-RAG -- three decision gates: classify if retrieval needed, grade doc relevance, check answer groundedness
queue.notes: Implements Asai et al. 2023. Model decides IF to retrieve, then grades each retrieved doc for relevance and support. Three conditional edges replace fixed retrieval.

queue.video_title: Parallel Subgraphs: Fan-Out and Fan-In with LangGraph Send API
queue.topic: Parallel subgraphs -- LangGraph Send API for true fan-out / fan-in across independent sub-tasks
queue.notes: Demonstrates the LangGraph 0.6.x map-reduce pattern using Send() for fan-out and Annotated[list, add] for fan-in. Critical for teaching parallel agent execution.

queue.video_title: LLM-as-Judge: Score RAG Outputs with a Rubric
queue.topic: LLM-as-judge eval harness -- score pipeline outputs on relevance, faithfulness, completeness
queue.notes: Self-contained eval loop using an LLM as the judge. Extends 16-rag-eval-notebook with a custom judge approach for teams without labeled data or a RAGAS budget.

queue.video_title: Agentic RAG: Let the Agent Choose When to Retrieve
queue.topic: Agentic RAG -- retrieval as one tool in a full ReAct loop alongside web search and a calculator
queue.notes: Agent decides per-turn which tool to call: vectorstore, DuckDuckGo, or Python eval. Unlike fixed-pipeline RAG, the agent can skip retrieval, chain multiple calls, or answer from memory.

queue.video_title: Multi-Agent Debate: Independent Answers + Critique Loop
queue.topic: Multi-agent debate -- two agents independently answer then critique each other to converge
queue.notes: Based on Du et al. 2023 (Society of Mind). Two solver nodes generate independent answers, exchange critiques for N rounds, a judge picks the winner. Contrast to example 6's routing pattern.

queue.video_title: Speculative RAG: Draft First, Retrieve Only to Verify
queue.topic: Speculative RAG -- draft answer first, retrieve only to verify and fix unsupported claims
queue.notes: Draft without retrieval; extract claims; retrieve evidence per claim; revise only unsupported parts. Faster path for questions the model can answer from training. Retrieval as fact-checker, not first step.

queue.video_title: DSPy: Auto-Optimize Your Prompts Without Hand-Crafting
queue.topic: DSPy-compiled RAG -- auto-optimize prompt signatures using MIPROv2 teleprompter
queue.notes: Framework contrast: DSPy Signatures declare what a prompt should do; MIPROv2 tunes few-shot examples from a small labeled dev set. No hand-crafted prompt engineering.

queue.video_title: OpenAI Agents SDK: Triage, Handoffs, and Built-In Tracing
queue.topic: OpenAI Agents SDK -- triage agent, specialist handoffs, structured tools, built-in tracing
queue.notes: Framework contrast: same multi-agent pattern as example 6 but using OpenAI's SDK primitives -- Agent, Runner, handoff() instead of StateGraph, nodes, conditional edges.

queue.video_title: Plan-and-Execute Agent: Structured Multi-Step Task Solving
queue.topic: Plan-and-Execute agent -- planner generates a typed step list, executor runs each step forward
queue.notes: Planner commits to a full plan before execution begins. Executor processes one step per iteration. Teaches DAG-style task decomposition vs monolithic ReAct loops. Contrast: planner never replans mid-run.

queue.video_title: Long-Term Memory: Persist User Facts Across Sessions
queue.topic: Long-term cross-thread memory -- agent stores and recalls facts across separate sessions using LangGraph InMemoryStore
queue.notes: Two-turn demo: turn 1 user shares facts, agent stores them; turn 2 runs in a new thread_id and recalls. Teaches checkpointer (within-thread history) vs store (cross-thread facts).

queue.video_title: ReWOO Agent: Plan All Tool Calls Upfront, Execute in Bulk
queue.topic: ReWOO agent -- generate all tool calls as a plan upfront, execute in bulk, synthesize (no interleaved observation)
queue.notes: Based on Xu et al. 2023. Planner emits all (tool, args) pairs at once; Worker executes in sequence; Solver synthesizes. Key contrast to ReAct: no observation interleaving = fewer tokens but less adaptive.

queue.video_title: LangGraph Command Pattern: Dynamic Routing Without Edges
queue.topic: LangGraph Command primitive -- edgeless dynamic routing where nodes return Command objects
queue.notes: Triage node + two specialist nodes with NO explicit edges. Routing is entirely via Command(goto='node_name', update={...}). Teaches LangGraph 0.6.x Command API as alternative to add_conditional_edges.

queue.video_title: Checkpoint and Resume: Survive Crashes with SqliteSaver
queue.topic: Checkpoint and resume -- persist workflow state to SQLite mid-run, resume from exact breakpoint
queue.notes: SqliteSaver checkpointer: run a multi-step agent, inject an interrupt after step 2, resume in a new Python process from the same thread_id. Teaches production-grade durable execution.

queue.video_title: Agent Observability: Token Counts, Latency, and Errors
queue.topic: Agent observability -- trace token counts, latency, and errors with callbacks and OpenTelemetry
queue.notes: Three observability layers: (1) LangSmith run tracing, (2) custom callback handler logging token usage per node to a DataFrame, (3) OpenTelemetry span export. Teaches cost-per-run and bottleneck detection.

queue.video_title: PDF Extraction Agent: Extract Typed Schemas from Any PDF
queue.topic: Structured PDF extraction agent -- pdfplumber reads PDF, LLM extracts typed schema, validator retries on failure
queue.notes: Three-node loop: reader extracts raw text, extractor populates a typed Pydantic model, validator routes back with a correction prompt if required fields are missing. Max 3 retries.

queue.video_title: Multimodal RAG: Index and Retrieve Images with GPT-4o
queue.topic: Multimodal RAG -- describe images with GPT-4o vision, embed descriptions in Chroma, answer image questions
queue.notes: For each image, GPT-4o vision generates a text description; embed the description; store in Chroma. At query time, retrieve top-k descriptions and pass image URLs to GPT-4o for final answer.

queue.video_title: Supervisor-Worker Multi-Agent: Dynamic Task Routing
queue.topic: Supervisor-worker multi-agent system -- supervisor LLM dynamically routes tasks to specialist worker agents
queue.notes: Supervisor uses with_structured_output to decide which worker to call (web_researcher, code_writer, fact_checker) or FINISH. Each worker is a sub-graph. Supervisor loops until FINISH or MAX_TURNS.

queue.video_title: MCP Client Agent: Discover and Call Tools at Runtime
queue.topic: MCP client agent -- LangGraph agent discovers and calls tools from a local MCP server over stdio
queue.notes: Spins up a local stdio MCP server exposing get_weather and unit_convert. LangGraph agent connects via MultiServerMCPClient, loads tool definitions at runtime, uses them in a ReAct loop.

queue.video_title: A2A Agent Handoff: Agent-to-Agent Protocol in Action
queue.topic: A2A agent handoff -- two LangGraph agents communicate over the Agent-to-Agent protocol
queue.notes: Orchestrator agent sends a research subtask to a Specialist agent running as an A2A-compatible HTTP service. Specialist returns a result card. Teaches the 2026 A2A protocol standard.

queue.video_title: Tree of Thoughts: Explore and Prune Reasoning Branches
queue.topic: Tree of Thoughts agent -- explore multiple reasoning branches in parallel, score and prune, select best
queue.notes: Proposer generates N candidate thoughts; Evaluator scores each; Selector prunes to top-k. Uses Send API for parallel branch expansion. Deliberative reasoning vs greedy chain-of-thought.

queue.video_title: DeepEval RAG Metrics: Faithfulness, Recall, and Precision
queue.topic: DeepEval RAG metrics -- Faithfulness, Answer Relevancy, Contextual Precision, Contextual Recall, Contextual Relevancy
queue.notes: Covers all 5 RAG-specific DeepEval metrics with math definitions. Builds ChromaDB RAG pipeline, runs deepeval.evaluate() on a golden dataset, shows failure modes and score breakdowns.

queue.video_title: DeepEval Safety: Detect Hallucination, Bias, and Toxicity
queue.topic: DeepEval safety metrics -- HallucinationMetric, BiasMetric, ToxicityMetric with deliberate failure injection
queue.notes: Deliberately injects hallucinated answers, biased phrasing, and toxic outputs to show scores respond correctly. Explains difference between Faithfulness (RAG-grounded) and Hallucination (standalone LLM).

queue.video_title: G-Eval: Write Custom Evaluation Criteria in Plain Language
queue.topic: G-Eval and custom metrics -- define any evaluation criterion in natural language with LLM-as-judge chain-of-thought
queue.notes: GEval(name, criteria, evaluation_steps) for fully custom criteria. G-Eval paper: CoT scoring with LLM judge correlates better with human judgment than ROUGE/BLEU. Also covers BaseMetric for deterministic checks.

queue.video_title: Evaluate AI Agents: Tool Correctness and Task Completion
queue.topic: DeepEval agentic metrics -- ToolCorrectnessMetric and TaskCompletion for ReAct agent evaluation
queue.notes: LangGraph ReAct agent with 2-3 tools. LLMTestCase with tools_called + expected_tools. Runs ToolCorrectnessMetric (right tools, right order?) and TaskCompletion. Shows why agent eval is harder than RAG eval.

queue.video_title: Conversational AI Evaluation: Multi-Turn Chatbot Scoring
queue.topic: DeepEval conversational metrics -- KnowledgeRetention, ConversationCompleteness, ConversationRelevancy
queue.notes: ConversationalTestCase with a list of LLMTestCase turns. Shows score drop when the bot forgets context after turn 3. Contrast turn-level vs conversation-level scoring.

queue.video_title: Auto-Generate Test Datasets and Run Regression Benchmarks
queue.topic: DeepEval Synthesizer -- auto-generate golden test datasets from documents, track score drift over time
queue.notes: Synthesizer generates goldens (question + expected_output + context) with SIMPLE, REASONING, MULTI-HOP, COMPARATIVE strategies. Run evaluate(), save/load EvaluationDataset, re-run after pipeline changes.

queue.video_title: Chunking Strategies: Fixed, Recursive, Semantic, and Sentence-Window
queue.topic: Chunking strategies notebook -- compare four chunking approaches side-by-side on the same corpus
queue.notes: Notebook comparing CharacterTextSplitter, RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, sentence-window (manual), and semantic (cosine boundary detection via OpenAI embeddings). Includes RAG quality comparison across all four strategies and an exercise on threshold tuning.

queue.video_title: Reranking RAG: Two-Stage Retrieval for Higher Precision
queue.topic: Cross-encoder reranking -- retrieve top-k candidates with vector search, rerank with a cross-encoder, pass top-n to generation
queue.notes: Three-node LangGraph: retrieve (Chroma top-20) -> rerank (FlashRank cross-encoder top-4) -> generate. Demonstrates bi-encoder vs cross-encoder tradeoff. flashrank added to requirements.txt.

queue.video_title: HyDE RAG: Retrieve with a Hypothetical Answer Embedding
queue.topic: HyDE RAG -- generate a hypothetical answer first, embed it instead of the raw query, retrieve with the richer embedding
queue.notes: Three-node LangGraph: hypothesize (LLM writes plausible answer) -> retrieve (embed hypothesis vector via similarity_search_by_vector) -> generate. No new deps. Teaches HyDE paper (Gao et al. 2022).

queue.video_title: Contextual Compression: Filter Retrieved Chunks Before Generation
queue.topic: Contextual compression -- filter retrieved chunks down to only the passage that answers the query
queue.notes: Three-node LangGraph: retrieve (Chroma top-8) -> compress (LLMChainFilter via ContextualCompressionRetriever) -> generate. Shows token reduction from raw to compressed context. No new deps.

queue.video_title: Step-Back Prompting: Abstract the Question, Then Retrieve
queue.topic: Step-Back Prompting -- abstract a specific question to a broader principle, retrieve on the principle, answer the original
queue.notes: Three-node LangGraph: step_back (LLM rewrites specific query to broader principle) -> retrieve (search on abstract query k=3) -> answer (LLM uses background context to answer original query). No new deps. Teaches Zheng et al. 2023.

queue.video_title: Tabular RAG: Answer Questions Over CSV Files with Pandas
queue.topic: Tabular RAG -- answer natural language questions over a CSV/Excel file using pandas code generation
queue.notes: Most RAG examples work on prose. This handles structured data. Three-node LangGraph: load_table (pandas read_csv, show schema), write_query (LLM generates a pandas expression given schema + question), execute_query (run expression in sandbox). On error, route back to write_query with error message. Sample dataset: 10-row product sales CSV inline in tools.py. Shows 3 questions: aggregations, filters, region totals. Contrast to 14-sql-agent. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: Semantic Router: Route Queries Without an LLM Call
queue.topic: Semantic router -- embed incoming queries and route to specialist nodes by cosine similarity, no LLM call needed
queue.notes: Routes code/billing/general via cosine similarity on pre-embedded descriptors. Threshold 0.6 fallback to general. 5 SAMPLE_QUERIES. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: Web Scraper Agent: Crawl, Extract, and Answer from Any URL
queue.topic: Web scraper agent -- crawl a URL, extract clean text with BeautifulSoup, chunk and answer questions
queue.notes: Fetch Wikipedia RAG article, parse with BeautifulSoup, chunk, embed into ephemeral Chroma, answer 3 questions. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: Guardrails Agent: Validate Input and Output with Pydantic
queue.topic: Guardrails agent -- validate user input and LLM output against Pydantic schemas, block violations, retry on failure
queue.notes: InputGuard + OutputGuard Pydantic schemas. 5 sample inputs (2 valid, 3 blocked: SSN, off-topic, email). Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: Async LangGraph: Concurrent Tool Calls with asyncio
queue.topic: Async LangGraph -- fully async nodes using ainvoke/astream, concurrent tool calls with asyncio.gather
queue.notes: All prior examples use synchronous .invoke(). This notebook shows the async path: async def nodes; graph.ainvoke() and graph.astream(); asyncio.gather() inside a node to call 3 tools concurrently. Show wall-clock time: sequential 3x1s=3s vs concurrent 1s. Explain when async matters: I/O-bound tasks (API calls, DB reads) benefit; CPU-bound (tokenization, embeddings) do not. Include async streaming: async for event in graph.astream_events(). No new deps.

queue.video_title: Token Budget Manager: Control AI Costs Per Run
queue.topic: Token budget manager -- track exact token usage per node, enforce a per-run budget, short-circuit gracefully when exceeded
queue.notes: 4-step neural network history research. BUDGET_LIMIT=800, tiktoken per step, short-circuit on budget exceeded. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: Pydantic AI: Typed Agents vs LangGraph
queue.topic: Pydantic AI -- build a typed research agent using the Pydantic AI framework as a contrast to LangGraph
queue.notes: pydantic_ai.Agent with result_type=ResearchResult (topic, summary, key_facts, confidence). No LangGraph -- direct run_sync. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: LiteLLM: Swap OpenAI, Anthropic, and Mistral Transparently
queue.topic: LiteLLM multi-provider -- swap models across OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral through a unified completion() interface
queue.notes: Add litellm to requirements.txt. Notebook showing: (1) litellm.completion() works identically for openai/gpt-4o-mini, anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5, mistral/mistral-small -- only the model string changes; (2) fallback chains: if primary provider errors, automatically retry on the next; (3) cost tracking via litellm.completion_cost(); (4) integrate with LangChain via ChatLiteLLM wrapper. Provider comparison table: cost per 1M tokens, context window, strengths. Exercises: run the same query on 3 providers and compare latency; simulate a 429 error and watch fallback trigger.

queue.video_title: SmolAgents: HuggingFace's Code-Executing Agent Framework
queue.topic: SmolAgents -- HuggingFace's minimal code-executing agent: LLM writes and runs Python directly as its reasoning trace
queue.notes: Add smolagents to requirements.txt. SmolAgents (HuggingFace, 2025): instead of defining named tools, the LLM writes Python code that imports and calls functions directly. Show a CodeAgent with web_search, text_summarize, and calculator tools where the agent's reasoning trace is runnable Python. Contrast table: LangGraph (nodes + edges + state) vs CrewAI (roles + tasks) vs SmolAgents (code generation + direct execution). Show the code execution sandboxing concern and how SmolAgents handles it.

queue.video_title: Mem0: Cross-Session Memory That Remembers Across Processes
queue.topic: Mem0 cross-session memory -- store and retrieve semantic + episodic memories that persist across completely separate processes
queue.notes: Add mem0ai to requirements.txt. Mem0 (MemoryAI, 2024) automatically extracts semantic facts and episodic memories using an LLM. Three-node LangGraph: recall_memories (m.search(query, user_id)) -> respond -> store_memories (m.add(messages, user_id)). Demo: session 1 user says 'I prefer Python'; session 2 in a fresh process recalls this without being told. Compare to 36-long-term-memory: Mem0 auto-extracts facts, LangGraph InMemoryStore is explicit. Include a memory dump showing raw Mem0 objects.

queue.video_title: Chain-of-Verification: Fact-Check Your AI's Own Answers
queue.topic: Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) -- generate an answer, enumerate verifiable claims, check each independently, revise failures
queue.notes: 4-node: generate, extract claims, verify each (ClaimVerification), revise failures. Einstein and Python biographies. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: Constitutional AI: Self-Critique Against Written Principles
queue.topic: Constitutional AI -- critique LLM output against a set of written principles, revise to comply, loop until satisfied
queue.notes: 5-principle constitution, generate→critique→revise loop, MAX_REVISIONS=3. 2 sample prompts. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: Prompt Injection Defense: Protect RAG from Malicious Documents
queue.topic: Prompt injection defense -- detect and neutralize indirect prompt injection in retrieved documents before they reach the LLM
queue.notes: 10-doc corpus with 3 planted injections. retrieve→classify_chunks→filter→generate. InjectionRisk high/low classifier. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: Parent Document Retriever: Small Chunks In, Full Docs Out
queue.topic: Parent document retriever -- index small child chunks for precise matching, return their full parent document for rich context
queue.notes: ParentDocumentRetriever + InMemoryStore. child=100 chars, parent=600 chars. 3 AI/ML questions. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: Batch Agent Runner: Process 100 Tasks Without Rate Limits
queue.topic: Batch agent runner -- process a list of tasks in parallel batches with rate limiting, retry backoff, and progress tracking
queue.notes: 9 tasks, BATCH_SIZE=3, asyncio.gather per batch, exponential backoff retry per task, summary table. Built 2026-06-12.

queue.video_title: LangSmith Tracing: See Inside Every Agent Run
queue.topic: LangSmith tracing integration -- decorate LangGraph nodes with @traceable, enable LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2, inspect runs in the LangSmith UI
queue.notes: Problem: agents are black boxes in production. Concept: LangSmith wraps every LLM call and node in a named trace with inputs/outputs/latency. Demo: @traceable on each node, set LANGCHAIN_API_KEY + LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true, run the agent, show the trace tree in LangSmith UI. Exercise: add custom metadata tags to traces.

queue.video_title: RAGAS Evaluation: Score Your RAG Pipeline in Code
queue.topic: RAGAS evaluation script -- build a QA dataset, run faithfulness and answer_relevancy metrics programmatically
queue.notes: Problem: notebook-only eval is hard to automate. Concept: RAGAS evaluate() takes a Dataset of (question, answer, contexts, ground_truth) rows and returns metric scores. Demo: build a 5-row QA dataset, run evaluate() with faithfulness + answer_relevancy, print score table. Extension: wire into CI to catch regressions.

queue.video_title: Streaming AI API: LangGraph + FastAPI Server-Sent Events
queue.topic: FastAPI SSE server -- stream LangGraph astream_events over HTTP as Server-Sent Events so any browser or client can consume token-by-token output
queue.notes: Problem: LangGraph streaming only works in Python scripts; real apps need HTTP. Concept: FastAPI StreamingResponse wraps an async generator that calls graph.astream_events() and formats each token as an SSE data: line. Demo: POST /ask, stream back token events, show curl consuming the stream. Extension: add a stop-sequence event type.

queue.video_title: Agent Evaluation: Score Your Agent Against a Golden QA Dataset
queue.topic: Agent evaluation with golden QA dataset -- exact match and cosine similarity scoring, pass rate summary
queue.notes: 5-pair golden QA set, single evaluate_one node, exact match + embedding cosine similarity scoring, per-question PASS/FAIL, avg score and pass rate summary. Built 2026-06-13.

queue.video_title: Langfuse Observability: Open-Source LLM Tracing
queue.topic: Langfuse agent observability -- self-hostable LLM tracing alternative to LangSmith; wrap LangGraph with Langfuse callback handler
queue.notes: SimpleTraceHandler mirrors LangfuseCallbackHandler interface (BaseCallbackHandler). ask_question -> format_answer 2-node graph, per-task trace IDs, llm_start/llm_end event capture. Built 2026-06-13.

queue.video_title: Prompt A/B Testing: Compare Concise vs Detailed Variants
queue.topic: Prompt A/B testing -- run two prompt variants on 5 inputs, score by word count, declare winner with comparison table
queue.notes: VARIANT_A (concise) vs VARIANT_B (detailed), 5 TEST_INPUTS, score_response = word_count/100, sequential run_variant_a -> run_variant_b nodes, printed comparison table with avg score and winner. Built 2026-06-13.

queue.video_title: LangGraph Studio: Visual Graph Debugging in Your Browser
queue.topic: LangGraph Studio local debugging -- visualize the graph, step through nodes interactively, inspect state at each checkpoint
queue.notes: Problem: debugging graph logic from print statements is painful. Concept: LangGraph Studio provides a browser UI to run, step, and replay graphs with full state visibility. Demo: open an existing complex graph in Studio, walk through node execution, modify state mid-run, re-run from checkpoint. Show how to write langgraph.json for local Studio.

queue.video_title: Production Supervisor Agent: Routing, Retries, and Fallbacks
queue.topic: Production-grade multi-agent supervisor -- dynamic worker routing with retry logic, worker health checks, and graceful fallback
queue.notes: Problem: example 43 has no error handling. Concept: production supervisors need retry logic when a worker fails and a fallback path when max retries are exceeded. Demo: inject a worker failure, show supervisor retry, show fallback to a simpler answer. Teaches defensive multi-agent design.

queue.video_title: Production HITL: Interrupt, Approve, and Audit Trails
queue.topic: Production human-in-the-loop -- interrupt() with audit logging, approval/rejection/edit paths, and a durable approval record
queue.notes: Problem: example 11 only does yes/no. Production needs approve/reject/edit plus an audit log. Concept: interrupt() captures the proposed action; user can approve, reject, or provide an edited version; all decisions are written to an audit JSONL file. Demo: agent proposes a database write, user edits the payload, approval is logged. Extends 39-checkpoint-resume for durability.

queue.video_title: Production Memory: Persist Agent State in Redis
queue.topic: Production memory with Redis -- replace InMemoryStore with a Redis-backed store for durable cross-session agent memory
queue.notes: USER_ID, REDIS_URL, SESSION_MESSAGES seeded into Redis list. load_history->respond->save_history nodes. 3 sessions show accumulated context. Built 2026-06-13.
